title: Behavior Pack Header
description: Defines the header section of a behavior pack manifest. Includes the pack's identity (name, description), its UUID, the version of the pack, and the minimum engine version required to load it.

tokens.text: defines the name, description, UUID, version, and minimum engine version for a behavior pack header

fields.title: Name
fields.description: Display name shown in the in-game pack list. Use a friendly title (e.g., 'My Add-on Starter') rather than the project's technical id.

fields.title: Description
fields.description: Multi-line description shown in the pack details panel. Explains what the pack adds.

fields.title: Pack UUID
fields.description: Unique 36-character identifier (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx). Two packs with the same UUID will conflict — generate a fresh UUID per pack.

fields.title: Version
fields.description: Semantic version of this pack: either a string like "1.0.0" or an array of three numbers like [1, 0, 0]. Bump when you ship updates so dependent packs/world saves can reference the right version.

fields.title: Minimum Engine Version
fields.description: The earliest Minecraft Bedrock engine version required to load this pack, as an array of three numbers like [1, 21, 0]. Older clients will reject the pack as incompatible.

fields.title: Pack Scope
fields.description: Optional. 'world' = the pack only applies inside a world (the default for most add-ons). 'any' = the pack can be loaded globally (typically only used for resource overrides or system packs).
